source: Debiasing Item-to-Item Recommendations With Small Annotated Datasets

concepts.finding: Bias in implicit feedback data: popularity bias, exposure bias, selection bias. Small high-quality editorial datasets can debias large-scale behavioral data. Importance sampling and inverse propensity weighting address exposure bias.
concepts.implication: Keyword co-occurrence data suffers from popularity bias (head keywords dominate), exposure bias (some intents are more visible), and selection bias (only observed co-occurrences count). Small curated keyword-intent pairs can debias large-scale behavioral co-occurrence.
concepts.open_question: How to debias keyword co-occurrence data for intent clustering? What are the sources of bias?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Inverse propensity weighting of keyword co-occurrence counts (downweighting popular keywords) improves intent cluster purity for tail keywords.
